Title: Ectopic expression of a rice triketone dioxygenase gene confers mesotrione tolerance in soybean

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes mesotrione first at the 5-carbon to form 5-hydroxy-mesotrione, and then performs a second oxidation leading to oxy-mesotrione-derived products such as xanthone-OH and acridone-OH derivatives, thereby detoxifying mesotrione.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly detoxifies the target herbicide and prevents inhibition of HPPD-dependent carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Confers herbicide tolerance by deactivating mesotrione and protecting carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers commercial-level mesotrione tolerance in soybean, with tolerance up to 16× commercial rate; prevents systemic mesotrione movement and protects carotenoid and chlorophyll accumulation in emerging leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in transgenic soybean under enhanced 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate trait gene for engineering mesotrione tolerance in soybean and potentially other dicot crops such as cotton; useful for stacking new herbicide tolerance modes of action.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: enhanced 35S promoter (constitutive); also mentioned different constitutive promoter pCon in supporting data
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf tissues directly exposed to herbicide; detoxified products retained locally in treated first trifoliate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper uses codon-optimized rice HIS1 expressed as TDO in soybean. Rapid local metabolism limits herbicide translocation to apex. Also confers tolerance to tembotrione and sulcotrione in supplementary data.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in soybean; herbicide injury assays; radiolabeled 14C-mesotrione translocation studies; HPLC/RAD/MS-MS metabolite profiling; UPLC-MS/MS quantification; carotenoid/chlorophyll measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Codon-optimized rice HIS1/TDO expressed in soybean confers commercial-level tolerance to mesotrione up to 16× rate. 2. TDO rapidly converts mesotrione in treated leaves into noninhibitory oxidized metabolites and conjugates. 3. This local detoxification prevents systemic herbicide movement to emerging leaves, preserves HPPD activity, and maintains carotenoid/chlorophyll biosynthesis under mesotrione treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Mesotrione foliar application at 1× to 16× commercial rate; radiolabeled 14C-mesotrione; 28/24 °C growth chamber conditions

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts hydroxyphenylpyruvic acid (HPPA) to homogentisic acid (HGA).
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tyrosine degradation / plastoquinone-linked carotenoid support pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for plastoquinone required for phytoene desaturase function and carotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step under herbicide inhibition context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic pigments protecting against bleaching and required for normal plant growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Protected indirectly in TDO soybean because mesotrione no longer accumulates in apex; in control soybean mesotrione inhibits HPPD, increasing HPPA and decreasing HGA.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function assayed in soybean shoot apex under mesotrione treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not the engineered focal gene here; serves as physiological target protected by TDO-mediated detoxification.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot apex for measured HGA effect; carotenoids in apex and first trifoliate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HPPD is the herbicide target rather than a transgene manipulated by the study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite measurements of HPPA and HGA in apex after mesotrione treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Mesotrione accumulation in conventional soybean apex inhibits HPPD. 2. HPPA rises and HGA falls in conventional apex, indicating enzyme inhibition. 3. TDO expression prevents mesotrione accumulation in apex and thus preserves HPPD function.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 2× mesotrione treatment; apex shielded during spraying
